reviews.review_description:
The user interface is phenomenal. It feels like this is the future of how we will play social games in vr. 
A virtual table projected in real life with your friends walking around it interacting.

The game is a lot of fun, highly recommended however I have some feedback for the developers 


- let me directly connect with my friends so we can play in person without their meta avatars moving around the table
-give us the ability to customize things like in worms. Let me change, weapon load out, weapon strength , basic things like disable trapped boxes which just randomly punish players and decrease the feeling of skill being the determining factor in who wins a match .
-the game weapons need to be balanced. Some items like gas grenades are so useless that they shouldn’t  count as a attack and seem pointless.
Other weapons are very powerful and you always want to use them, while others you never will touch unless you’re out of everything else.

in worms the weapons are well balanced so they all have their uses. 

Keep up the great work i hope you give players the tools to play the game the way they want!

This game is easily 5 stars. Wonderful interactions, fun and funny, great graphics. The only downside to this game is the lack of player base for lobby's. Any and all I have played with, young and old, has been a great fun time. I teach as others do as well the dynamics of how this game works. But, when I create a lobby,  it becomes truly hit and miss if you will have a game  or not.

I haven't fully tried out the game yet but from all the reviews I've seen there is no sandbox mode. I really enjoy sandbox games and was wondering if whenever you can or whenever you want you can add a sandbox
I'm going to give it a three right now because I haven't really checked out the game but I will check it out later

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! We highly recommend checking out our Skirmish Mode when you play. It lets you freely customize the level, game rules, weapon sets, and reinforcement rules for a real sandbox feel.

If there's a specific feature you mean by 'sandbox mode' that we're missing, feel free to elaborate. we're always listening to player ideas!

reviews.review_description: This game is great. Brings back retro PC vibes. It's chill and fun. Would be nice if there were more solo campaigns though as there usually aren't many open lobbies for multiplayer.

I've owned Table Troopers for a while now, and it captures the spirit of classic Worms really well in VR.
The game offers a fun campaign, solid AI battles, and the overall gameplay feels great also just for single players.
The only drawback: the multiplayer lobby is often quiet. :-(

Still, this game deserves more attention. I hope more players discover it so the multiplayer can grow

When they can just lob grenades from the other side of the playing area that always land right beside you, it doesn't feel all that balanced. Chuck in being outnumbered and having a sniper up yer harris and it just gets a bit tiresome.

developer_response.body:
First of all, thanks for taking the time to give us feedback.
I hear that you are not happy with the difficulty level of the game. There are two main modes to play the game in single-player: Skirmish matches or the campaign. In skirmish matches against the computer, you can choose one of three difficulty levels to make the game easier or more challenging.
In the campaign, we go for a more puzzle-style approach where you have to use your equipment the right way to beat enemies that feel overpowered on first sight. For example: build walls for cover, collect weapons, plan a few moves ahead, and sometimes you just need a little luck.
If you have any additional feedback on how we can make the game more appealing to you or have feedback to specific levels, we would be happy if you join our Discord or write us an email.

reviews.review_description:
If you've played the game Worms on PC or console, this game is essentially that but in VR.  Table Troopers is tremendous fun and there are plenty of laughs to be had as you discover how certain weapons work (including the inevitable environmental mishaps that can result).

My hope is that the devs will read this and consider a few suggestions that would make this an easy 5 star review:

1) Allow 2x2 team play with humans and/or bots.
2) Better table movement and control, including the ability to tilt the board.  Having table control similar to Demeo would be wonderful and considerably more intuitive.
3) Allow players to have independent control over the table rotation.  When it's another player's turn, I'd like to be able to rotate the table to see what they are doing.
4) Better difficulty scaling or maybe 5 bot skill levels rather than 3.  At medium, bots seem to miss way too much, and at high skill, they can hit a perfect shot from across the map in strong wind.

For the price, this game is great and easy to recommend, but with a few tweaks, it could be OUTSTANDING.
try this game

reviews.review_title: Bug with Pirate ship map in Multiplayer
reviews.review_description: This is really a funny game and we enjoy it! Though it has some bugs that we encountered while playing Multiplayer. In example the Pirate ship map get stuck when playing multiplayer, nobody can move or shoot. Devs please take a look and fix it. Thanks! Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body: The latest update should fix the issue. Thanks again for taking the time to report this.

reviews.review_description: Titulo
The game is amazing, but I believe the Ragdoll mechanic would add much more excitement, especially during the explosions!
Hoping more people pick this up. It's a ton of fun to play with friends!

reviews.review_title: Very fun but with a few downsides
reviews.review_description:
Has real potential and is very fun up to a point but:
(1) Needs an option to turn off the annoying character voices that interrupt you every 10-15 seconds and demand you do something while trying to think about your move.  Its so distracting that Ive had to turn off sound completely.   Its like being surrrounded by a bunch of ADHD toddlers.
(2) Needs a way to grab move the whole board like Demeo etc rather than just turn in place or do a whole VR reset.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback! Always appreciated.
(1) As a first step, we can set the timers a bit higher before they make themselves noticeable, and add an option to toggle them off later on.
(2) You can move the board around with the thumbstick on the left controller, and move the table up and down with the X/Y buttons. We also have a one-handed mode on our to-do list that will enable dragging the board with the grab button.
We’ll try to add these changes in one of the next updates.

reviews.review_description: A relatively simple game that I have been spending so much time on! I just can't seem to put it down. Very reminiscent of the old game worms on PC. A must-have and the price is right! I would love to see more levels for one player, and was pleasantly surprised when the holiday levels came out!

A very entertaining VR game that is easy to get into and fun to play with friends. The mini-battles are funny and fast-paced, and don't require any complicated skills. If you're looking for a casual but enjoyable VR experience, this is the perfect choice.

Hello,
the game is great and I'm really enjoying it. I just have one question about multiplayer: would it be possible to play the single player campaign mode while searching for an opponent?
The wait for the next player to connect is sometimes longer and this would be a nice way to use this time.
Thank you very much for your answer and have a nice day 🙂
Braňo

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your feedback! We actually have a feature on our todo list that gives you a notification when someone opens a lobby while you are playing in single player. We will release it in one of the next updates

reviews.review_description: It's similar to worms lots of fun!!!

The game really shines in multiplayer. Seeing other player's avatars move around the map and strategize, then react when shots land or miss, is hilarious. It takes the gameplay of a worms-like turn-based strategy game, and makes it feel like you're gathered around a tabletop board game. So fun, and I can't wait to see how it evolves with updates.

reviews.review_title: love it, don't want to stop playing!!!!
reviews.review_description: So fun, jumped on and don't want to stop. Addicting, will be purchasing the extra campaigns. Cant wait to play w/ friends
We own a few headsets in our house and play this game every week! Thank you devs for making a great tabletop Works style  game in 3D!

reviews.review_title: Had my eye on this game for ages
reviews.review_description:
Sooo good to play, gives me a childhood vibe, like worms but better!

It's so chill and fun to play, everything has been well thought out and put together.

I hope more people start playing so I can get some online multiplayer action!

However even single player campaign is great 😎

I love that there's DLCs and everything, I hope it's continuing to be developed.
 Great game thank you devs
Great game!! Glad there was an option to turn off MR lol

Ridiculous fun. Lemmings VR. Highly tactical turn based excellence. Mixed reality means i can still watch tv between turns 🤗. Plenty maps and weapons to use. Sniper rifle got me bobbing my head around trying to get the perfect shot 🤣
Just playing the tutorial and I was hooked. Brought me back to playing 🪱's with the guys
Fun game. A blast to play with my son. Pun intended.

I still enjoy playing this game. One of my most visited games in VR. Love joining with my friend and others online and when you're not in the mood the bot play is just as fun. It's a must for everyone in VR. I don't have an issue with any of the mechanics at all which I usually find in most other games. 10 out of 10!!!
Great game, I can't wait to see what they do with it.

This is going to be one of them games that I play for many years. If you like Worms this is basically that in 3D VR. Just like with Worms it shines when playing multiplayer. Hopefully we continue to get new content. A super trooper (sheep) would be cool. Maybe we can get bigger tables down the line, or tables with a roof could be implemented somehow. Not sure if it could work on a table top but just an idea. Then you could add ninja trooper ropes which would add so many different gameplay styles.

There's not much to add that other people haven't already said. It's essentially a worms clone and fairly well done. I haven't tried multiplayer yet so can't comment, but if you're looking for that style of game you'll probably like it.

I personally would like to see some changes to the controls. The single button, select from menu setup just adds unnecessary steps that could be handled with other buttons. While aiming the dotted line fades at a distance which can make for some difficulty with longer shots because you're susceptible to variations in multiple axes, unlike in a 2D game. And I found myself restricted to holding my hand inside the circle of the play space when aiming certain shots where it was needed to pull back, which felt restrictive and unnatural - the ability to extend beyond the border would be much better. And resizing would be a great addition.

So, it loses a little in the rating for me personally until it's a bit more intuitive to use. Overall a decent game anyone can pick up and play.
Very entertaining

reviews.review_title: A Fun Challenge Even In Single Player
reviews.review_description: I've only tried single player so far.  I've found the AI to be challenging enough.  It's not so bad as to be frustrating, but you'll need to think through your moves and will feel appropriately accomplished for each victory earned.  I love that it can be played seated and in MR.  This is great on the headset when I don't feel like being too active.  Also the devs continue to support and update the game.
Definitely a good game to play with friends!!!

Been having a lot of fun with this over Christmas with my mates.

Message to developers - Keep up the great work ! But PLEASE let us name our teams and characters !!! That is a MUST. PLEASE !!!

I personally love watching these little fellas get blown off the map and then falling to their deaths, where they remain on my floor for the rest of the game. The difficulty is spot on, not overly easy and you have to plan out your movements and attacks. 
I’d love to see more campaign levels/maps, weapons, costumes. Keep up the great work!

Ive been playing VR since 2017, Ik a thing or two. Recently, I bought this game for my Q3 and Q2 to play between my wife and I. Let me just say….WE ARE ADDICTED!! This game works in AR perfectly on Q3 and Q2. We enjoy lining up our avatars over each other and battling out!! Lots of maps, multiple players to choose from, and tons of weapons. It’s already becoming a MUST HAVE on the quest. This game is simply a masterpiece for next level VR tabletop games. Hats off to the developers 🧢

What an amazing game. The MR experience is great with the table in the middle of my play area. The campaign was exciting to play, just a bit short for my taste. I hope the developers add more levels/chapters soon. It would also be great to have 4-5 difficulty levels to choose from (with more/smarter/stronger enemies), then even replaying the campaign at a higher difficulty level would be fun.

So far, I only played in single mode, and I expect the game to be even better when playing with friends. Congratulations to the developers for this very well made gem 👍🏻
Well worth it, especially for the price.  They need to add the ability to search for a multi-player game while playing a single player game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! We will release a feature next year that gives you a notification when a new lobby is opened while you are playing single player!

reviews.review_description: One of the best games going
If you don’t like playing games that’s makes you run around your home, try this.
What a good little game, great fun for a great blast.

If you like Worms You will love it. 

Only thing i wish would be added is a few maps with moving vehicles as Hazzards, like a highway with speeding cars or train yard, or space with spaceships flying around. Or birds Flying around to block your shot if your unlucky.  eitherway great start to your game guys.  Amazing price Btw thank you.

These little characters have become like members of our family, and ohhhh, do I let them down all the time with my terrible aim.  And I do have terrible aim, but this is one of those rare games that never frustrates me.  I just want to try, try again.  The graphics and the animations and the sound effects are just a symphony of feel-good joy.  I feel like this game could heal a childhood.  As a person with terrible aim, this game gives me something most games don't: the gift of patience.  There are no knee-jerk, rapid-fire responses.  I get a turn, a chance to target my opponent without being fired upon.  And while others take their turns, the strategy and the anticipation of what I will plan or do next just stirs in my head.  I think this game is amazing at any age or skill level.  The accessibility features are the most thoughtful, as I can move/rotate the table physically by grabbing it or by using my buttons to raise/lower or my thumbstick to move around or even inside the table for a better POV while taking aim (or to watch my opponents take their shot).  And if it sounds simple, it isn't!  If I use the wrong weapon, I could easily blow my own character backward (sometimes to their doom!) from the kickback.  There are some decent physics that really get my brain going.  And I have to mention that the character options and their pun-ny names are adorable and always bring a huge smile to my face, and we can even play under one of several different challenging environments.  There is an option to play in mixed reality or full immersion as well.  For someone like me, with arthritis and who struggles with hand fatigue... I can play this for hours because the game gives me the tools and the time.  If you buy 1 game to play (alone or with friends), this is the one!
It's starts off slow but you have to give it time. Great fun and addictive.
The best with good friends. Old worms vibes coming up. Great for all ages!

1- Why on earth do you disable right thumbstick rotation when spectating?
2- When a player is aiming, the other's movement/spectating, they should be invisible/not able to interfere (often unintentionally)
3- Please add some balance for odd player number games, like: team A+B can team up against  C, but C has twice the players.

reviews.review_title: One of the best MR games on Quest 3
reviews.review_description: It's just so cool to have a virtual table in your room to play a nice Worms 3D-like game.  Overall it feels very polished and plays great. Also, it has a nuke, so +1* for that. I'm sure more weapons and other stuff will be added in the future. If some sort of ranking or stats will be implemented and the online community will grow, this game will be a 5* for me.

Love tbe game but the issue is with multiplayer. There is no way to see players that you don't know. You cant open a room and wait for someone to join you for a game. 
Consequently I cant search for anyone who has opened a room and is looking for a game with a random. 
This is a total bummer and should be sorted out asp.

I love playing so far with my gf. Reminds me of Xcom. I wish maps were bigger so instant 1st move takedown werent possible by knocking them into the water. A character field of view only for spotting enemies would be awesome. The option to have even more characters on-screen. Anything that makes it more similar to Xcom imo would be amazing.
fun lay back game

Growing up I used to play Worms quite a bit. I heard that Table Troopers had a similar style, but I wasn't expecting to love it this much! Not only does it do everything that Worms did well - it also adds VR mechanics that make me want to keep coming back for more. If this game had Quest Achievements, it may very well he my VR Game of the Year. The fun-factor is definitely there.

reviews.review_title: Ok, but the AI is bush league
reviews.review_description: The solo campaign is just pure luck at some point. Ai guys sniping mine from all the way across the board before they even get a turn... nah man, too frustrating for me. Don't understand all these 5 star reviews, guess our standards here on quest are pretty low.
Love the game but would be awesome if you could play 2 vs 2 or 2 vs 1 vs 1  but love it otherwise

Worth every penny! My wife and I played this non stop for hours and just can't get enough. I really hope to see continuous updates on maps, characters and overall gameplay. I believe new maps will keep this game fun and exciting.

Its worms armageddon but 3d.  Very well made and updated.

Cons: 
Needs more players, most ice seen online is 30.
Maybe needs something like worms had where you can personalize your team.
Once u Finnish campaign, best played with others. Lobby is very quiet🙁

If you like mixed reality games like meta showed in the quest 3 ad, then get this game. It’s not too fast paced, or slow. It’s perfect and fun, you don’t need to know a million different things to play and they did a really good job on the game. The graphics are amazing, the sounds are good, the gameplay is smooth and intuitive. So if you want a game worth your money. Get this game!

Pros 
Multiple weapons options 
Cool maps
Fun gameplay 
Cute characters 
Cons 
Using the sniper rifle across the whole map is way to difficult 
AI aiming is way to precise in solo play 
Throwing grenades in wind is too difficult.

Its rather worms 1 than worms 2.critics on high niveau: 
-personally i would prefer real movement over dummy placement
-if i use a weapon without moving, there is no advantage (like shooting twice?)
-some small retreat movement would be nice. 

Overall it is angeaging, animations are nice and fluid and i love the atomic explosion from the airstrike 😈👍

reviews.review_title: VR perfection in a Worms-like game
reviews.review_description:
This game is such a joy.  You’ve got an absolutely brilliant VR control scheme layered on top of some immensely satisfying physics & destruction, all wrapped up in a game that already rivals Worms with its charm. As a kid playing Worms I could have never even dreamed of actually playing something as cool as this.

My suggestions for things I’d love to see in the future:

- Team customization tools
- Ragdoll physics with the pawns when they die would be very satisfying
- Hand tracking, if it can work well enough
- Map creation tools
- Procedurally generated maps maybe?

I really don’t have any complaints beyond some minor performance things and animation stutters here and there but I know it’s Early Access so that stuff will get ironed out I’m sure.

Really well done, devs! You’ve got a banger of a game already and I can’t wait to see how it develops!

This games plays to the worms fans great iteration on the turn based format bit small maps great implementation on how to fire the weapons pull back back mechanic works great all in all devs done this game type justice the appeal to a certain player base cheap in todays market vr must play with friends who like to sit down and play games

reviews.review_title: TOP, FUN AND EXPLOSIVE! THE VR WORM!
reviews.review_description:
Table Troopers achieves the perfect blend of fun and explosive arcade gameplay and turn-based strategy. It alternates between massive shooting, gadgets, and soldier placement in MR or VR.
A great time to share with friends, but it also offers a very fun single-player mode.
Table Troopers is accessible with simple controls... but random results!
Place the game on your table, choose your target, aim the bazooka, and boom, you're in for a laugh.
The weapons are varied (bazooka, grenades, air strike, etc.), and their handling, combined with the physics, creates unpredictable moments where even a missed bullet provokes laughter.
The game offers options to adjust the game (weapons, wind, reserve), which helps you fine-tune the strategy to suit your tastes.
The single-player mode is already fun, but the real appeal is the multiplayer mode: there's nothing like a surprise explosion thrown by a friend to trigger laughter. For the modest price of €10, Table Troopers is a sure bet among tactical VR games, perfect for evenings with friends or short strategy sessions.
I can only recommend this title, which, for me, easily earns the title of "hit"!

reviews.review_title: Worth 3x the price for the fun we've had
reviews.review_description:
Excellent. The levels are varied, the weapons are fun and the 'juiciness' of the interface is absolutely off the chart - whoever is responsible for the physics of the interface knows their stuff. Strange to be talking about? Sure, but it's the attention to detail extending as far as the UI that shows the care that's been applied across the whole - it's so well made. 
Excellent game, the team deserve a huge hit for the work they've put in here. Can't wait to see what else they do with the levels going forward.

Table Troopers perfectly captures the spirit of those classic Worms battles I loved as a kid, but puts you right in the center of the action with VR. Those moments when you think you’ve lined up the perfect shot, only to watch it backfire spectacularly, are what make this game pure fun for me. Whether I’m blasting through the campaign or facing friends online, every match delivers those “did-that-just-happen?” moments. Table Troopers is an absolute must-have. Don’t miss out!

Loving the game so far. I grew up playing lots of worms, so it scratches that itch. One thing, the trajectory line is really difficult for me to see. It would be greatly beneficial for colorblind users (or users that would appreciate higher contrast) to be able to change the color and or contrast of the line. Some of these long shots are almost impossible to see when the line starts to fade.

developer_response.body: We just pushed an update to improve playability for players with color blindness. Starting with game version 2025.38.19, you can now adjust the color of the aim preview, and we’ve also updated the player colors for better visibility. If you have further suggestions, don’t hesitate to reach out to us by email or Discord.

reviews.review_description:
I’ve been looking for a game to have fun, chat and spend time with friends. Table Troopers ticks ALL those boxes! 

If you’ve ever played Worms, then you’ll get how to play this game instantly. It still brings enough innovation to make it unique though! 

Graphics are great and it plays really well. It’s well worth the price!

pretty much perfect. incredibly fun to play in mixed reality with friends. 

could really do with  either a map maker or randomly generated maps as the selection of levels for multiplayer seemed pretty limited. I would also like to see more weapons in the game. 

at the price the developers charged for it I would gladly pay for map packs.  

currently one of my favourite games on quest
My 14 yr old and I go Headups on this...Definitely WORTH IT!!!...Open update too

reviews.review_title: Great game, like worms in VR
reviews.review_description: Can you add ragdoll physics to the death animations please? It would make it way more entertaining because the default animation is not very satisfying.

reviews.review_title: Nice chill way to blow off some stress
reviews.review_description: I have a couple of coworkers that also have Quest headsets so we are always looking for fun, multi-player games to play.  Other games like golf, bowling, etc are fun but there is something about being able to slap a co-worker in the face with a fish that is just so satisfying! Co-worker throws you under the bus at work ?  Take them out with a sniper shot from long range.  Better yet, bomb them from the air.  So many different types of weapons but we have so much fun and laughter every time we play.  This is not a violent game despite the game play.   Best part is "Slap you with a fish" has become part of our office speak in our group.  We get a few weird looks but laugh among ourselves.  It's a great game so just get it and have fun.
Awesome! Hopefully the AI gets better but its a lot of fun. Thr mixed reality is a cool effect.

I found out about this game from A Wolf in VR, and I immediately bought it. This is such a cool concept and it has a ton of potential if the devs provide fresh content occasionally.

So far I have only played solo. It's fun, but it certainly makes you realize that this game is meant to be played with others, but for the price I paid, I have no regrets.

As of now, it's not easy to find a random lobby, and the Discord is pretty quiet so far, but if you have friends to play this with, I can say with certainty that you will have a blast blasting each other in this game.

Highly recommend (especially if you have friends)!

A VR/MR turn-based strategy game that'll have you laughing as you take out your foes with everything from super pineapples, airstrike nukes, a big fish slap to the face, or good old-fashioned bazookas.Think Worms, but you can view the action from any angle even up close and personal. You'll be battling it out with not just the other teams but also things like nasty winds & rising sea levels. 😬

At a mere $9.99 USD, Table Troopers is an absolute steal. It's wildly fun, highly addictive, and worth every single penny. Seriously, go play it! Surviv0r tested, Surviv0r approved!

Purchased this game.  Initially it would not download at all.  With help from Support, received it. What a waste.  Poor instructions and ultimately just a lesson in frustration and disappointment.  Used a gift card to purchase. Waste of a gift.

Table Troopers is a vibrant, tabletop-style VR strategy game that nails the essentials right from the start. It’s intuitive, smooth, and impressively well-crafted. The tutorial guides you in with perfect pacing, making it easy to learn while staying fun.

The characters—heroes and enemies alike—are charming and memorable. Actions are quick to select and feel precise, allowing for smooth, satisfying gameplay. The art style is colorful and cartoony, adding extra charm to every match.

Multiplayer mode brings the real chaos and fun. If you’re nostalgic for games like Worms, this is your next must-play. Don’t hesitate—dice into this absolute gem of a game!

I’m so glad I bought this well designed and has the one more try magic about the gameplay.
The nukes great fun!
Well done to the devs👍🏻

This really scratched an itch even the official Worms3D never did. The game feels just a little small, like the original worms, where more weapons, etc, could happen - and with 3 dimensions to spread things out chain reactions are a little less entertaining/long, but they do happen.
The game relies on movement a bit more than Worms, enforced by generally being short on decent weapons and wanting crates. I've yet to find a "big" weapon that can do a lot of damage in one hit yet - not too far off from the original but it would be nice to have I think.
I've also had a repeatable issue where if turn-length is set to infinite the game will hang, so just set it for 1 minute or you'll get frustrated having to quit mid match all the time.
Lots of fun, good remote or in person.
Two suggestions:
1) Let users rotate the table when it is not their turn, so they can see the action!
2) SAVE custom settings - each time I have to manually turn it from default to custom, then go set two pages of settings to my likings. They should reappear as I last used them.

Again, great fun, *highly* recommended.
Great game for passthrough mode. I can play while doing other tgings. Airstrikes are so fun and they keep upgading what you can do in passthrough.

reviews.review_title: Best way to hang out with friends online
reviews.review_description:
Table Troopers has quickly become one of my favorite ways to connect with friends remotely. Before this, I played a lot of VR minigolf just to hang out with people across the country, but Table Troopers takes that social experience to a whole new level.

The graphics are awesome, and the UI is really intuitive. It never gets in the way, which makes it easy to just focus on the fun. There's also a lot of mayham in the best way possible—chaotic, hilarious moments that always lead to good laughs with friends.

Table troopers is the closest thing to worms in vr and it's surprisingly fun. The gameplay stays true to worms’ original spirit: quick turns, creative use of terrain, and a variety of weapons. VR and MR well implemented with simple and intuitive controls for movement and aiming. Perfect for fans of social VR and turn-based games!
